schedule.description: Athletics rally past Cubs — series goes 0-2

stories.title: Athletics Win Game 2 — Cubs Fall to 32-30, Swept So Far in Series
stories.angle: NEW STORY (game recap — morning after June 3 loss; Rea vs Springs, tied 4-4 through 8, Athletics rallied late)

x_posts.label: Post 1A
x_posts.text:
Game 2 slipped away.

Cubs and the A's were knotted at 4 into the final innings last night — then the bullpen couldn't hold it. Athletics win, Cubs fall to 32-30 and trail the series 0-2.

Imanaga has to be different tonight. 7:05 PM CT.

#Cubs #GoCubs #FlyTheW

stories.title: Bregman's Hot Stretch — .303 BA, .361 OBP, 20-Game On-Base Streak in May
stories.angle: FOLLOW UP (Bregman featured June 2 as streak angle; fresh hook: May final 18 games stats + streak ended in June 3 0-4 game — write as "what he built" for June momentum)

x_posts.label: Post 2A
x_posts.text:
Alex Bregman has been locked in since mid-May.

.303 BA, .361 OBP, and a 20-game on-base streak through the month of May. After a slow start, this is the version of Bregman the Cubs signed.

He's the hottest bat in this lineup right now.

#Cubs #CubsBaseball #MLB

stories.title: Imanaga Must Deliver Tonight — Series Finale, 7:05 PM CT at Wrigley
stories.angle: NEW STORY (Imanaga start preview; hasn't had a standalone preview this week)

x_posts.label: Post 3A
x_posts.text:
Cubs need Imanaga to be great tonight.

Down 0-2 against the 30-31 A's, this is a sweep-or-survive game. Imanaga is Chicago's best arm — and there's no better time for an ace to show up.

7:05 PM CT. Wrigley. Let's see it.

#Cubs #GoCubs #FlyTheW

stories.title: June Schedule Is Still Cubs' Best Shot — Giants, Rockies, Jays, Mets Are Next
stories.angle: FOLLOW UP (June favorable schedule from June 1; fresh angle: even after dropping 2 to the A's, the window is not closed — upcoming schedule is still soft)

x_posts.label: Post 4A
x_posts.text:
Yeah, the Cubs just dropped 2 to the A's. But here's the thing:

Giants, Rockies, Blue Jays and Mets are all next — NONE above .500.

If Cabrera and Boyd come back healthy, this rotation goes deep fast. The June window is still wide open.

#Cubs #NorthSiders #MLB

stories.title: Rotation Relief — Cabrera Returns Saturday, Boyd Close Behind
stories.angle: FOLLOW UP (rotation reinforcements covered June 3; fresh angle: confirmed Saturday start for Cabrera, Boyd 2nd rehab start same day with imminent activation)

x_posts.label: Post 5A
x_posts.text:
Rotation update for Cubs fans:

Edward Cabrera (3-2, 4.00 ERA) returns to the mound Saturday vs the Giants. Matthew Boyd makes his 2nd Iowa rehab start Saturday — activation expected shortly after.

Two arms. Five days. The cavalry is almost here.

#Cubs #CubsBaseball #MLB

research.facts:
Rotation Reinforcements — Cabrera Saturday, Boyd Close
**Source freshness:** <48h (ESPN story June 2-3; Last Word on Sports June 4)
**Confirmed facts:**
- Edward Cabrera placed on 15-day IL May 24 with right middle finger blister
- Cabrera season stats before injury: 3-2, 4.00 ERA
- Cabrera expected to start Saturday vs Giants (Wrigley, June 7)
- Matthew Boyd had 2nd Iowa rehab start Saturday (June 7 or earlier — date unclear)
- Boyd: left knee surgery recovery; Counsell: "good to go" after 2nd rehab start
- Jordan Wicks optioned (11 runs, 13 hits in 6.1 IP over 2 starts)
- Tyler Ferguson recalled
**Sources:**
- ESPN: "Cabrera, Boyd close to returning to Cubs' rotation"
- Chicago Sun-Times June 2: Cubs rotation depth article

stories.title: Matt Shaw Begins Iowa Rehab — Return to Wrigley Lineup on Horizon
stories.angle: NEW STORY (Shaw rehab started June 3; first coverage of this specific development)

x_posts.label: Post 6A
x_posts.text:
Matt Shaw is rehabbing at Triple-A Iowa.

He's been out 16 days with mid-back tightness, but looked healthy in his first Iowa rehab game. If all goes well, he could be back in the Wrigley lineup sooner than expected.

More depth incoming.

#Cubs #GoCubs #ChicagoCubs

stories.title: Brewers Lead the NL Central — Cubs 6+ Games Back, But June Can Change That
stories.angle: FOLLOW UP (NL Central standings updated; Cubs fell further after June 3 loss, Brewers remain strong)

x_posts.label: Post 7A
x_posts.text:
The Brewers lead the NL Central and aren't letting up.

Cubs sit at 32-30, 6+ games back. But Giants, Rockies, Jays, and Mets are all next — none above .500. This is exactly what the soft June schedule is for.

Start tonight.

#Cubs #NorthSiders #MLB
